prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for aniline were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS, density-fitted) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry was taken from the user-supplied aniline.xyz (no re-optimization). Fukui indices were derived from Mulliken charge differences between the neutral (N), cation (N−1, charge +1, mult 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, mult 2) states. All three SCF calculations converged. Integrity: trustworthy = true. No warnings were reported; all integrity checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000, schema_header present).

Headline results:
- Most nucleophilic site: Atom 1 (N), f⁻ = 0.20452359
- Most electrophilic site: Atom 3 (C, ortho), f⁺ = 2.2975867

The nitrogen lone pair is the dominant nucleophilic centre; ortho/para ring carbons show the highest electrophilic susceptibility, consistent with –NH₂ activating the ring toward electrophilic aromatic substitution.

CLI invocation: cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard aniline.xyz
